decks.notes: The main idea of this deck is to use Balustrade Spy to mill your entire library, putting all your creatures into the graveyard. Then, you can use Dread Return to bring back Lotleth Giant and deal damage to your opponent equal to the number of creatures in your graveyard. The deck also utilizes cards like Saruli Caretaker, Wall of Roots, and Overgrown Battlement to ramp up your mana and quickly fill your board with creatures. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help you dig through your deck to find your combo pieces.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana through artifacts and sacrificing them for powerful effects. By utilizing cards like Refurbished Familiar and Myr Enforcer, it aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures. Galvanic Blast and other burn spells provide additional reach to finish off the opponent. With a mix of card draw, removal, and aggressive creatures, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and control the board to secure victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the pace of the game by utilizing Gates to fix mana and provide extra card draw. It includes a mix of removal spells, counter spells, and creatures to maintain board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to survive the early game with efficient blockers like Sacred Cat and Squadron Hawk, then stabilize and take control with powerful late-game threats like Basilisk Gate and Guardian of the Guildpact. The synergy between Gates and various spells in the deck allows for flexibility and adaptability in different matchups.

The main idea of this deck is to aggressively attack the opponent using low-cost creatures and direct damage spells. It capitalizes on the Rakdos colors of black and red to quickly deplete the opponent's life total. The deck also includes cards that provide additional value such as card draw and lifegain, while maintaining a consistent mana base. By continuously applying pressure and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the Rakdos Aggro deck aims to overwhelm and defeat the opponent before they can stabilize.

The focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy centered around Elves. Using efficient mana dorks like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to ramp into powerful threats like Timberwatch Elf and Nyxborn Hydra, the deck aims to flood the board with creatures quickly. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help refuel the hand with more Elves, while Masked Vandal and Avenging Hunter provide removal and disruption. Priest of Titania generates massive amounts of mana to power out even more Elves, creating a relentless and synergistic aggro strategy.

The focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using cheap burn spells like Lightning Bolt, Lava Dart, and Fireblast, while also benefitting from creatures like Sneaky Snacker and Guttersnipe. Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery help generate card advantage and provide additional damage, while Kessig Flamebreather and Voldaren Epicure offer consistent damage output. Faithless Looting helps to dig for key cards and fill the graveyard for Fireblast. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of burn spells and aggressive creatures.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost creatures and disruptive spells. It uses counterspells and bounce effects to control the board while also filling the graveyard to reduce the cost of powerful threats like Cryptic Serpent and Lórien Revealed. By applying pressure early and countering key spells, the deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize. The card selection and card advantage provided by Brainstorm, Ponder, and Thought Scour help to maintain tempo and find necessary pieces for victory.

The main strategy of this deck is to control the game by utilizing Ephemerate to blink creatures with powerful enters-the-battlefield effects, such as Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer, while also protecting them with counterspells like Counterspell. This allows for card advantage and board control, while also potentially gaining life from Crypt Incursion. The deck also includes removal spells like Skred and Fanged Flames for dealing with threats, and card draw spells like Preordain to keep the player's hand full. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to outvalue and outlast the opponent through efficient control strategies.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive red creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to play fast and hit hard, using cards like Burning-Tree Emissary and Goblin Bushwhacker to swarm the board. Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt provide additional reach to finish off the opponent. Rally at the Hornburg can help replenish the hand and keep up the pressure. The deck aims to end the game quickly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize or mount a defense.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board with counterspells and removal spells while also utilizing low-cost creatures to chip away at the opponent's life total. With a combination of Faeries, Spirits, and other creatures, this deck aims to apply consistent pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By combining aggression with disruption, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory in a Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a quick and aggressive strategy using Elves. By efficiently ramping up mana with cards like Elvish Mystic and Priest of Titania, and generating card advantage with Lead the Stampede and Winding Way, the deck aims to deploy powerful Elf creatures like Timberwatch Elf and Nyxborn Hydra to quickly close out the game. The deck also includes support cards like Quirion Ranger and Masked Vandal to provide additional utility and disruption. The goal is to swarm the board with Elves and establish a dominating presence to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing a combination of counterspells, card draw, and disruptive creatures. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while efficiently deploying threats like Cryptic Serpent and Tolarian Terror. Lórien Revealed provides valuable card advantage, while Thought Scour and Mental Note help dig deeper into the deck. Sleep of the Dead can be used to disrupt the opponent's board state, while Deem Inferior provides additional removal. The deck's mana base consists solely of Islands to ensure consistent access to blue mana.

This Pauper 1 vs 1 Control Gates deck focuses on utilizing a combination of gate cards and control spells to manage the board and disrupt opponents' strategies. By utilizing cards like Journey to Nowhere and Prismatic Strands, along with creatures like Sacred Cat and Outlaw Medic, the deck aims to survive early aggression and establish board presence. The inclusion of Faithless Looting and Pursue the Past helps maintain card advantage and consistency. Overall, the deck aims to stall the game while gradually gaining an advantage through its gate synergy and control elements.

The deck focuses on controlling the game with a mix of counterspells, removal, and card draw. It utilizes the powerful interaction between Ephemerate and creatures like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer to generate value and keep control of the board. The deck also includes some burn spells and card advantage engines to help close out the game. Overall, the key strategy is to outvalue and outlast the opponent through a combination of control elements and card advantage.

The main focus of this deck is to generate a large amount of mana quickly using cards like Desperate Ritual, Seething Song, and Geothermal Crevice, in order to cast high-impact spells such as Big Score and Highway Robbery. Goblin Anarchomancer and Thornscape Familiar help reduce the cost of spells, while cards like Electric Revelation and Pirate's Pillage provide card advantage. The goal is to assemble the necessary pieces for the Ruby Storm combo, which can deal lethal damage to the opponent in a single turn.

The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with low-cost, aggressive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent, supported by cheap utility spells like Counterspell and Dispel to control the board. Card draw and manipulation spells like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Thought Scour help fuel the aggression by ensuring a consistent stream of threats. Lórien Revealed and Deem Inferior provide additional ways to boost the power of the deck's creatures, while Sleep of the Dead can lock down opposing threats. Deep Analysis offers some additional card advantage in longer games.
